description: Common string operations and formatting. Contains constants for character classes, a Template class for simple substitution, and a Formatter class for advanced string formatting.

functions.name: capwords

params.name: sep
params.description: Word separator. If None, runs of whitespace are replaced by a single space and leading/trailing whitespace is stripped.

functions.description: Split the argument into words using str.split, capitalize each word using str.capitalize, and join the capitalized words using str.join. If the optional second argument sep is absent or None, runs of whitespace characters are replaced by a single space.

functions.name: maketrans

params.name: to
params.description: Characters to map to. Must be the same length as from.

returns.description: A 256-character translation table suitable for str.translate()

functions.description: Return a translation table suitable for passing to str.translate(). Each character in from will be mapped to the character at the same position in to. from and to must have the same length.

classes.name: Formatter
classes.description: The Formatter class implements the same layout as the format() built-in. It allows customization of the formatting behavior by overriding its methods.

methods.name: format

params.name: format_string
params.description: The format string containing replacement fields

methods.description: The primary API method. Takes a format string and an arbitrary set of positional and keyword arguments. Calls vformat().

methods.name: vformat
methods.description: Does the actual work of formatting. Accepts args and kwargs as a tuple and dict respectively, rather than unpacked.

methods.name: parse

returns.description: Iterable of (literal_text, field_name, format_spec, conversion) tuples

methods.description: Loops over the format_string and returns an iterable of tuples (literal_text, field_name, format_spec, conversion). Used by vformat() to break up the format string into literal text and replacement fields.

methods.name: get_field

params.name: field_name
params.description: The field name as returned by parse()

returns.description: Tuple of (obj, used_key) where obj is the resolved value

methods.description: Given a field_name as returned by parse(), convert it to an object to be formatted. Returns a tuple (obj, used_key). The default version calls get_value() then handles attribute and index lookups.

methods.name: get_value

params.name: key
params.description: The key (integer index or string name)

methods.description: Retrieve a given field value. If key is an integer, it indexes into args; if it is a string, it looks up in kwargs.

methods.name: check_unused_args

params.name: used_args
params.description: Set of keys/indices that were referenced in the format string

methods.description: Called after formatting is complete. Override to check for unused arguments and raise an exception if desired. The default implementation does nothing.

methods.name: format_field
methods.description: Format a single field. The default implementation calls the global format() built-in.

methods.name: convert_field

params.name: conversion
params.description: Conversion type: 's' for str(), 'r' for repr()

methods.description: Convert a value given a conversion type (as returned by the parse() method). The default implementation handles 's' (str), 'r' (repr), and None (no conversion).

classes.name: Template
classes.description: A simpler string substitution mechanism. Templates use $-based substitutions: $identifier or ${identifier}. Use $$ for a literal dollar sign.

params.name: template
params.description: The template string using $-based substitution syntax

constructors.description: Create a new Template with the given template string.

methods.name: substitute

params.name: **kwds
params.description: Keyword arguments used as substitution values

methods.description: Perform substitution, returning a new string. Raises KeyError or ValueError if a placeholder is not found in the mapping and no keyword argument matches.

methods.name: safe_substitute

params.name: **kwds
params.description: Keyword arguments used as substitution values

returns.description: The substituted string with missing keys left intact

methods.description: Like substitute(), except that if placeholders are missing from the mapping and kwds, the original placeholder is left intact in the resulting string rather than raising an exception.

fields.name: template
fields.description: The original template string passed to the constructor

constants.name: whitespace
constants.description: A string containing all characters considered whitespace: space, tab, newline, return, formfeed, and vertical tab

constants.name: printable
constants.description: String of characters considered printable: digits, ascii_letters, punctuation, and whitespace

constants.name: letters
constants.description: Concatenation of lowercase and uppercase. Locale-dependent in Python 2.7.

constants.name: lowercase
constants.description: A string containing all characters considered lowercase letters. Locale-dependent in Python 2.7.

constants.name: uppercase
constants.description: A string containing all characters considered uppercase letters. Locale-dependent in Python 2.7.
